message:
test(fuzz): add ZoneId parser fuzz target (resolves kzahd)

Adds fuzz_zone_id_parse covering ZoneId::from_str and
ZoneId::from_tailscale_tag. ZoneId is the zone-access security
primitive — every capability, object, and event in FCP is scoped to
exactly one zone — and the validate path (capability.rs:537+) has
eight rejection branches:

  - empty
  - length cap (64 bytes)
  - non-ASCII
  - missing 'z:' prefix
  - empty segment
  - reserved 'z:proj-' prefix
  - project-zone leading/trailing dash
  - char allowlist [a-z0-9:_-]

No fuzz target previously existed. Properties asserted:

  1. ZoneId::from_str is panic-free over arbitrary UTF-8.
  2. Accepted parses preserve the canonical form byte-for-byte —
     the validator must not transform inputs.
  3. Round-trip through to_tailscale_tag → from_tailscale_tag yields
     the original zone, except where to_tailscale_tag's documented
     '_' → '-' rewrite (capability.rs:515,517) is intentionally lossy.
  4. ZoneId::from_tailscale_tag is panic-free, and every accepted
     output passes ZoneId::from_str — the two parser paths agree on
     what counts as a valid zone.
  5. Standard zones (owner/private/work/community/public) are always
     accepted by from_str and round-trip unchanged — anchors the
     fuzzer to the documented zone hierarchy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-71lku","title":"[REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-host: public /rpc/cancel trusts spoofable X-Principal header","description":"file: crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:2693-2695, 4208-4235; crates/fcp-host/src/cancellation.rs:168-180

repro:
1. Start any authenticated invoke for principal user:test and capture its operation_id while it is still in flight.
2. From a second caller with no bearer auth and no capability token, POST /rpc/cancel (or /rpc/operations/cancel) with that operation_id and header X-Principal: user:test.
3. The public router dispatches cancel_handler, which forwards only the caller-supplied header principal into CancellationController::cancel.
4. cancellation.rs accepts the plain string match and cancels another principal's operation.

why this is real:
- /rpc/cancel and /rpc/operations/cancel are mounted on the public app router, not the protected_routes router guarded by admin_auth_middleware.
- cancel_handler does not require a capability token, session proof, or admin bearer; it trusts only extract_principal_header(&headers).
- CancellationController::cancel authorizes by plain string equality on the stored owner.

suggested fix:
- require authenticated cancellation proof (for example the same live capability-token principal model as invoke, or a dedicated signed cancel token), or move the cancel route behind a real auth layer instead of trusting X-Principal alone.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T00:46:41.744995Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T01:04:25.627791Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T01:04:25.627293Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2896,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-71lku","author":"jemanuel","text":"Fixed in commit e2cb9fc5 (cc_2). Moved /rpc/cancel + /rpc/operations/cancel from public app router into protected_routes, gated by existing admin_auth_middleware (Bearer token + owner zone header). 3 regression tests added: spoofed X-Principal alone → 403, /rpc/operations/cancel alias → 403, wrong bearer → 403. Tests use tower::ServiceExt::oneshot to exercise the full routing layer. Behavior change noted in commit message: tenant self-cancel via plain X-Principal no longer works; restoring tenant self-cancel without the bypass requires threading a capability_token through CancellationRequest (larger refactor, separate scope).","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:04:25Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-7ca","title":"fcp.sentry: Release tracking and deployment correlation","description":"# fcp.sentry: Release tracking and deployment correlation (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable agents to correlate production changes with incidents by accessing:
- releases
- deployments
- release health

## Scope
### Read operations
- List releases for a project/org with filters.
- Get release details:
  - version
  - commits (when available)
  - associated issues
  - deployment environment timeline
- Optional: fetch release health summaries (sessions, crash-free users).

### Write operations (optional; dangerous)
- Create a release marker or deployment marker.
  - Typically CI-owned; allow only if policy explicitly enables.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `sentry.releases.read`
- `sentry.releases.write` (dangerous; optional)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Avoid leaking commit metadata or PII via logs.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `sentry.release.list`
  - `sentry.release.get`
  - `sentry.release.health_summary`
  - Optional: `sentry.release.create`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - list/get pagination
  - association parsing
  - rate limiting + retry/backoff
  - capability gating for any write ops

## Acceptance criteria
- Agents can reliably answer: “what changed before this incident?”
- All writes (if enabled) are approval/capability gated and audited.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-cdj","title":"fcp.1password: Credential Management Connector","description":"## Overview
Secure credential management connector for 1Password integration, enabling AI agents to safely access and manage secrets without exposing sensitive data.

## Core Capabilities

### Item Lookup (Read-Only by Default)
- Search vault items by name, tags, or categories
- Retrieve specific fields (username, URL, notes) without exposing passwords
- Read-only access as the default permission level
- Explicit elevation required for password retrieval

### Password Generation
- Generate strong passwords with configurable requirements
- Support for length, character sets, and complexity rules
- Memorable password generation (word-based)
- PIN generation for numeric-only contexts

### Entry Creation
- Create new login entries with generated passwords
- Support for secure notes, credit cards, identities
- Custom field support for application-specific data
- Automatic categorization and tagging

### Vault Organization
- List and navigate vault structure
- Move items between vaults
- Bulk tagging operations
- Archive and restore items

### Secure Note Access
- Read secure notes for configuration data
- Structured note parsing (YAML, JSON embedded)
- Document attachment retrieval
- Version history access

## Security Requirements

### Credential Protection
- **CRITICAL**: Never expose credentials in logs or output
- Mask all sensitive fields in debug output
- Use secure memory handling
- Clear credentials from memory after use

### Time-Limited Credential Leases
- Short-lived access tokens (5-minute default)
- Automatic credential rotation
- Lease renewal with re-authentication
- Audit trail for all credential access

## Implementation Notes
- Use 1Password CLI (op) or Connect API
- Support both personal and team accounts
- Service account integration for automation
- MFA handling for interactive sessions

## Testing Requirements
- Mock vault for unit tests
- Integration tests with test vault
- Security audit for credential handling
- Penetration testing for leak prevention","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:21:48.804778945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-15T07:34:56.145167808Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T07:34:56.145167808Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of flywheel_connectors-8egw","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-cga","title":"fcp.spotify: Podcast Episode Data Access","description":"# fcp.spotify: Podcast Episode Data Access (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide access to podcast metadata for discovery and summarization workflows.

## Scope
- Get show metadata.
- List show episodes (pagination).
- Get episode metadata.
- Optional: saved episodes list (ties to library mgmt).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `spotify.podcasts.read`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Treat titles/descriptions as tainted external content.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `spotify.show.get`
  - `spotify.show.episodes.list`
  - `spotify.episode.get`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - market handling

- connectors/aws/src/connector.rs:170 (aws_sigv4_supported: false)
- connectors/aws/src/connector.rs:295-296 (placeholder X-Aws-* headers)
- connectors/aws/src/client.rs:67 ('simplified REST without SigV4 signing')
- connectors/aws/manifest.toml:14 (describes the limitation)
- connectors/s3/src/client.rs:454-465 (placeholder presigned URL)

CURRENT STATE:
The AWS connector sends custom X-Aws-Access-Key-Id, X-Aws-Secret-Key, X-Aws-Region headers instead of real SigV4-signed requests. The connector explicitly tracks this limitation (aws_sigv4_supported: false, missing_sigv4_overrides vector, doctor warnings). Default AWS endpoints reject all traffic. The S3 connector's presign_url() generates a placeholder URL with a comment noting 'Real AWS SigV4 signing would require the full AWS SDK or manual SigV4 implementation.'

OPTIONS:
A) Full SigV4 implementation: Implement AWS Signature Version 4 signing per https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/sigv4_signing.html. This is ~500-800 lines of crypto (HMAC-SHA256 canonical request, string-to-sign, signing key derivation). Could use the aws-sigv4 crate as a dependency.
B) Minimal SigV4 via aws-sigv4 crate: Add aws-sigv4 as a dependency (small crate, MIT licensed). This provides SigningSettings, SigningParams, and sign() function. ~50 lines of integration code.
C) Gate with honest errors: Change invoke() to return FcpError::NotImplemented with message 'AWS SigV4 signing is required for this operation but not yet implemented. Configure *_base_url overrides to use a signing proxy.' Remove the placeholder headers entirely.

RECOMMENDATION: Option B (aws-sigv4 crate) for the AWS connector, Option C as an interim gate for S3 presigned URLs (SigV4 presigning is a separate, more complex flow).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AWS connector either signs requests with SigV4 or returns an explicit unsupported error (no silent placeholder headers)
- S3 presign_url() either generates real presigned URLs or returns an error
- doctor() accurately reports SigV4 support status
- All existing tests pass
- New tests verify SigV4 signing with mock AWS endpoint or verify explicit error on unsigned requests","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:39Z"},{"id":1974,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-kdmf0","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose verified: AWS connector already comprehensively gates SigV4 limitation. aws_sigv4_supported=false is tracked, missing_sigv4_overrides vector identifies which endpoints need override URLs, doctor() reports the limitation with actionable fix instructions, self_check() refuses to probe default STS without SigV4, and the manifest.toml documents the constraint. S3 presigned URL comment is documentation-honest. No fake success responses are returned — the connector either uses endpoint overrides (signing proxy) or reports clear errors. The gating satisfies the bead's 'gate operations behind explicit unsupported error' criterion. Full SigV4 implementation via aws-sigv4 crate remains desirable future work but is not blocking correctness.","created_at":"2026-03-25T04:54:07Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-kipeo","title":"[FCP3/P2] Carve kernel, policy, and evidence into first class owners","description":"Background: V3 requires separate owners for execution semantics, policy semantics, and evidence semantics.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-zkj2d","title":"[REVIEW][MEDIUM] fcp-registry/lib.rs: signed package symlinked binary bypasses standalone-file check","description":"file: crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:2100-2149

repro:
1. Create a signed package directory with a valid manifest/signature and a real binary file inside the package directory.
2. Replace the declared signature.binary_name path with a symlink inside the package directory that points to that real sibling file.
3. Load the package through LocalRegistryCatalog::from_signed_package_dirs().
4. The loader canonicalizes the path and then uses std::fs::metadata() on the resolved target, so the symlink passes as a regular file as long as it stays inside the package directory.
5. That contradicts the LinkedBinary contract that the binary must be a standalone regular file.

suggested fix:
Use symlink_metadata() on the originally joined binary path and reject symlinks before following them, then keep the existing traversal and hard-link checks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:04:14.002416Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T01:15:30.421993Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T01:15:30.421669Z","close_reason":"Rejected symlinked signed package binaries and added regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-zp1xx","title":"[REVIEW-P4][MEDIUM] fcp-crypto::CwtClaims::constraints_cbor panics on invalid CBOR","description":"crates/fcp-crypto/src/cose.rs:223-235 documents that constraints_cbor takes pre-serialized CBOR, but it deserializes with ciborium::from_reader(cbor_bytes).expect("constraints_cbor: invalid CBOR"). Bug scenario: any caller that forwards malformed or attacker-supplied constraint bytes into this public builder path can abort the process instead of getting a typed CryptoError. This is especially dangerous because the rest of the COSE/CWT surface is fail-closed and error-returning, so a single malformed constraints blob becomes a panic denial-of-service instead of a normal token-construction failure. Suggested fix: make constraints_cbor return CryptoResult<Self> (or add a fallible companion and deprecate the panicking variant) and reject invalid CBOR without unwinding.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:33:00.235836Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:41:22.434941Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:41:22.432735Z","close_reason":"Added fallible CwtClaims::try_constraints_cbor + CapabilityTokenBuilder::try_constraints_cbor returning CryptoResult<Self> with CryptoError::SerializationError on invalid CBOR. The existing panic-prone constraints_cbor is now #[deprecated] AND no longer panics — it tracing-warns and leaves the constraints claim UNSET on malformed input, so a legacy caller's worst-case outcome is a token without constraints that the verifier's C3.4 constraints-required gate rejects at verify time, strictly better than a process abort. Added tracing as a production dep of fcp-crypto. 4 new regression tests (malformed CBOR rejection via try_ variant, valid CBOR acceptance, builder wrapper propagation, legacy shim no-panic behavior).
